description: Authenticated user POSTs to /articles/:slug/favorite on an existing article they have not favorited, and the article is added to their favorites.
expected: 200 with body { article } where article.favorited === true, article.favoritesCount incremented by 1, and article includes author and tagList fields; the join row now exists in the DB.

description: Authenticated user DELETEs /articles/:slug/favorite on an article they previously favorited, removing it from their favorites.
expected: 200 with body { article } where article.favorited === false and article.favoritesCount decremented by 1 relative to the favorited state.

description: Authenticated user POSTs favorite twice on the same article to verify the Sequelize addUser association does not duplicate the favorite.
expected: Both requests return 200; after the second request article.favoritesCount is still 1 (not 2) and article.favorited === true.

description: Request POST /articles/:slug/favorite with no Authorization header, so verifyToken passes through without setting loggedUser and the controller rejects.
expected: UnauthorizedError is passed to the error handler (401 response); no state change to favorites.

description: Authenticated user POSTs favorite for a slug that matches no article.
expected: NotFoundError("Article") is passed to the error handler (404 response); no join rows created.

description: Send POST favorite with an Authorization header lacking a token after the scheme (e.g. just "Token"), triggering the malformed-token branch in verifyToken.

citation.snippet: if (!token) throw new SyntaxError("Token missing or malformed");

expected: SyntaxError is forwarded to the error handler and the request fails with a non-2xx error response; controller is never reached and no favorite is created.

description: Send POST favorite with an Authorization header containing a token that fails jwtVerify.
expected: Error("Invalid Token") is forwarded to the error handler and the request fails with a non-2xx error response; no favorite state change.

description: Two different authenticated users favorite the same article; the second user's response must report the total count across all users while favorited reflects only the requesting user.
expected: Second user's 200 response has article.favoritesCount === 2 and article.favorited === true; a subsequent DELETE by user one returns favoritesCount === 1 with user one's favorited === false.
